segments.text:
Introduction to carbonyls (C=O)
Types of carbonyls
The MO of carbonyl reactivity
Nucleophilic addition of cyanide to aldehydes and ketones
Preview of other aldehyde and ketone reactions
Addition of hydride (makes alcohol)
Addition of organometallic reagents (makes alcohol with new C-C bond)
Addition of water (makes hydrate)
Addition of alcohols (makes acetals and hemiacetals)
Introduction to acid and base catalysis with carbonyls
Organic Chemistry 2 Lectures CHEM-C 342, Spring 2021
Week 3 Lecture Slides – Week of 2/1/2021
Instructor: Kasey J. Clear

Formaldehyde
Formaldehyde: toxic & bp = -21 °C Store as a solid (form formaldehyde upon heating): paraformaldehyde trioxane ( mp = 62 °C)
Store as a liquid (formalin, 40% aq. solution):

Hydration of ketones and aldehydes
Thermodynamic stability of hydrates
Carbonyl K eq
Ketones 	    10 -4 -10 -2
Aldehydes	        ~1
Formaldehyde       40
Chloral	       500
